body_template.query: { deployments(input: { projectId: "$refs.project_id" }, first: 50) { edges { node { id status } } } }
{ environments(projectId: "$refs.project_id", first: 100) { edges { node { id name } } } }
{ project(id: "$refs.project_id") { services { edges { node { id name } } } } }

filter.description: Filter logs by a string. Providing an empty value will match all logs.

limit.description: Limit the number of logs returned (defaults 100, max 5000)

execution.graphql_query: query($deploymentId: String!, $limit: Int, $filter: String, $startDate: String, $endDate: String) { buildLogs(deploymentId: $deploymentId, limit: $limit, filter: $filter, startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) { timestamp message severity } }

filter.description: Filter logs by a string. Providing an empty value will match all logs.

limit.description: Limit the number of logs returned (defaults 100, max 5000)

execution.graphql_query: query($deploymentId: String!, $limit: Int, $filter: String, $startDate: String, $endDate: String) { deploymentLogs(deploymentId: $deploymentId, limit: $limit, filter: $filter, startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) { timestamp message severity } }
query($input: DeploymentListInput!, $first: Int, $after: String) { deployments(input: $input, first: $first, after: $after) { edges { node { id status createdAt staticUrl creator { name } } } } }

domains.description: All domains for a service instance

applyChangesInBackground.description: If true, the changes will be applied in the background and the mutation will return immediately. If false, the mutation will wait for the changes to be applied before returning.

skipInitialDeploys.description: When committing the changes immediately, skip any initial deployments.

sourceEnvironmentId.description: Create the environment with all of the services, volumes, configuration, and variables from this source environment.

stageInitialChanges.description: Stage the initial changes for the environment. If false (default), the changes will be committed immediately.

environment_logs.description: Fetch logs for a project environment. Build logs are excluded unless a snapshot ID is explicitly provided in the filter

afterDate.description: Latest date to look for logs after the anchor

afterLimit.description: Limit the number of logs returned after the anchor

anchorDate.description: Target date time to look for logs

beforeDate.description: Oldest date to look for logs before the anchor

beforeLimit.description: Limit the number of logs returned before the anchor

filter.description: Filter logs using a query syntax

environment_triggers_deploy.description: Deploys all connected triggers for an environment.

environments.description: Gets all environments for a project.

github_repo_branches.description: Get branches for a GitHub repo that the authenticated user has access to

github_repos.description: Get a list of repos for a user that Railway has access to

metrics.description: Get metrics for a project, environment, and service

averagingWindowSeconds.description: The averaging window when computing CPU usage. By default, it is the same as the `sampleRateSeconds`.

endDate.description: The end of the period to get metrics for. If not provided, the current datetime is used.

groupBy.description: What to group the aggregated usage by. By default, it is grouped over the entire project.

includeDeleted.description: Whether or not to include deleted projects in the results

sampleRateSeconds.description: The frequency of data points in the response. If the `sampleRateSeconds` is 60, then the response will contain one data point per minute.

startDate.description: The start of the period to get metrics for.

botPrEnvironments.description: Enable/disable pull request environments for PRs created by bots

focusedPrEnvironments.description: Enable focused PR environments that only deploy services affected by changed files

projects.description: Gets all projects for a user or workspace.

execution.graphql_query: query($id: String!) { service(id: $id) { id name icon updatedAt } }

service_connect.description: Connect a service to a source

branch.description: The branch to connect to. e.g. 'main'

image.description: Name of the Dockerhub or GHCR image to connect this service to.

repo.description: The full name of the repo to connect to. e.g. 'railwayapp/starters'

environmentId.description: Environment ID. If the specified environment is a fork, the service will only be created in it. Otherwise it will created in all environments that are not forks of other environments

templateId.description: Template ID. Required when templateServiceId is provided.

templateServiceId.description: Template service ID within the template's serializedConfig. Required when templateId is provided.

environmentId.description: [Experimental] Environment ID. If the environment is a forked environment, the service will only be deleted in the specified environment, otherwise it will deleted in all environments that are not ...

service_disconnect.description: Disconnect a service from a repo

environmentId.description: [Experimental] Environment ID. If the environment is a fork, the service will only be updated in it. Otherwise it will updated in all environments that are not forks of other environments

replace.description: When set to true, removes all existing variables before upserting the new collection.

variables.description: All variables by pluginId or serviceId. If neither are provided, all shared variables are returned.

serviceId.description: Provide a serviceId to get all variables for a specific service.

volume_create.description: Create a persistent volume in a project

environmentId.description: The environment to deploy the volume instances into. If `null`, the volume will not be deployed to any environment. `undefined` will deploy to all environments.

mountPath.description: The path in the container to mount the volume to

projectId.description: The project to create the volume in

region.description: The region to create the volume instances in. If not provided, the default region will be used.

serviceId.description: The service to attach the volume to. If not provided, the volume will be disconnected.

volume_delete.description: Delete a persistent volume in a project

volume_instance.description: Get a single volume instance by id

volume_update.description: Update a persistent volume in a project

certificateErrorMessage.description: Human-readable error message when certificate issuance fails

certificateErrorType.description: Structured error type for programmatic handling

certificateRetryable.description: Whether the certificate issuance can be retried

deploymentStopped.description: Check if a deployment's instances have all stopped

attributes.description: The attributes that were parsed from a structured log

message.description: The contents of the log message

severity.description: The severity of the log message (eg. err)

tags.description: The tags that were associated with the log

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the log message in format RFC3339 (nano)

ts.description: The timestamp of the sample. Represented has number of seconds since the Unix epoch.

tags.description: The tags that were used to group the metric. Only the tags that were used to by will be present.

hasHiddenRegistryCredentialsFromTemplate.description: Whether this service has hidden registry credentials from a template. When true, the credentials are stored in the template and used during deployment.

has2FAEnforcement.description: Whether 2FA enforcement is enabled for this workspace.

hasAutomaticDiagnosis.description: Whether automatic deployment diagnosis is enabled for this workspace.

hasGuardrailsAccess.description: Whether this workspace has access to guardrails policies.

redactedDueTo2FAPending.description: Whether the current user's access is redacted due to pending 2FA requirement. Returns true if the user is a workspace member, workspace has 2FA enforcement enabled, and the current user needs to en...

usersWithout2FA.description: Get a list of user emails in the workspace who do not have verified 2FA enabled. Returns an empty array if all users have 2FA enabled.
